name: Bill Haley
description: American rock and roll music pioneer (1925–1981)
abstract: William John Clifton Haley (; July 6, 1925 – February 9, 1981) was an American rock and roll musician. He is credited by many with first popularizing this form of music in the early 1950s with his group Bill Haley & His Comets and million-selling hits such as "Rock Around the Clock", "See You Later, Alligator", "Shake, Rattle and Roll", …

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] When Bill Haley and Elvis came on the scene, you would have been about 9 or 10. So that makes sense. [INTERVIEWEE] That’s about right, yeah
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Elvis Presley, I remember Bill Haley and the Comets and all that, it just never did anything for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: my sister... had all these records of all these guys... Bill Haley and the Comets
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with the original Comets so it’s basically everybody who was on the original record apart from Bill Haley... Anyway so it was basically the original band that toured with Bill Haley.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Naundorf
about_quotes.evidence: And then was also Bill Haley very well. ... Bill Haley and Scum. Yeah, that was the beginning. And so far my money was reached, I bought me then only the plates.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: we stayed over there a month with Bill Haley and his band. 'Course I didn't sing with Bill Haley. I sang with the other band that was with us on the tour.
